business.name: Carolina Arcade Museum
business.tagline: Classic arcade and pinball games all on Freeplay

voice.ownerQuote: You have more feedback, and more fun when you're playing with somebody, and you're there physically. It's more than just a screen you're looking at.

content.heroHeadline: Classic Arcade and Pinball. All on Freeplay.
content.heroSubtext: Over 100 vintage arcade and pinball machines in downtown Forest City. Pay once, play all day.

pricing.name: All-Day Pass
pricing.description: Play all day, come and go

birthdayParties.includes: Party room for 2 hours, 15 all-day wristbands
birthdayParties.extras: Additional wristbands $8 each. Bring your own food and decorations.

testimonials.quote: Great place to bring the family and enjoy the whole day.
Awesome place for you to relive your childhood.
This place is so much fun for everyone!
It was so much fun, we will be visiting again soon.
A hidden fun spot for indoor activities. Forest City is an adorable little downtown.

content.story: Dave Walker and a friend opened Carolina Arcade Museum in August 2018 in a converted storefront on Main Street in downtown Forest City. With over 100 classic arcade and pinball machines, all set to freeplay, the museum recreates the social experience of 1980s arcade culture. No tokens, no quarters. Just pay at the door and play everything.
sellingPoints: 100+ classic arcade and pinball machines, all on freeplay
One price, unlimited play, come and go all day
Featured on PBS North Carolina Weekend
Downtown Forest City on Main Street
